titles.ar: باب بَعْضِ الْوَرَثَةِ يُقِرُّ بِعِتْقٍ أَوْ دَيْنٍ
titles.en: Chapter 28 – Some the of the inheritors acknowledge the emancipation or debt

titles.ar: باب بَعْضِ الْوَرَثَةِ يُقِرُّ بِعِتْقٍ أَوْ دَيْنٍ
titles.en: Chapter 28 – Some the of the inheritors acknowledge the emancipation or debt

segments: رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ عَبْداً فَشَهِدَ بَعْضُ وُلْدِهِ أَنَّ أَبَاهُ أَعْتَقَهُ قَالَ يَجُوزُ عَلَيْهِ شَهَادَتُهُ وَ لَا يُغْرَمُ وَ يُسْتَسْعَى الْغُلَامُ فِيمَا كَانَ لِغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ .
arabic.text: رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ عَبْداً فَشَهِدَ بَعْضُ وُلْدِهِ أَنَّ أَبَاهُ أَعْتَقَهُ قَالَ يَجُوزُ عَلَيْهِ شَهَادَتُهُ وَ لَا يُغْرَمُ وَ يُسْتَسْعَى الْغُلَامُ فِيمَا كَانَ لِغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin marrar, from Yunus, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who died and left a slave, so one of his children testified that his father had emancipated (liberated) him. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘His testimony is allowed over it, and he would neither be made to compensate nor would the slave have to work for it with regards to what was for the others from the inheritors’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin marrar, from Yunus, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who died and left a slave, so one of his children testified that his father had emancipated (liberated) him. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘His testimony is allowed over it, and he would neither be made to compensate nor would the slave have to work for it with regards to what was for the others from the inheritors’.

segments: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ’Isma‘il ibn Marrar from Yunus from Mansur ibn Hazim who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who dies and leaves behind a slave and certain ones of his children testify that their father had freed the slave. He (the Imam) said, ‘His testimony is permissible, he is not required to compensate and the slave is not required to work to pay for his freedom from the other heirs.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ’Isma‘il ibn Marrar from Yunus from Mansur ibn Hazim who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who dies and leaves behind a slave and certain ones of his children testify that their father had freed the slave. He (the Imam) said, ‘His testimony is permissible, he is not required to compensate and the slave is not required to work to pay for his freedom from the other heirs.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/72)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/72)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مَرَّارٍ عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي

titles.ar: باب بَعْضِ الْوَرَثَةِ يُقِرُّ بِعِتْقٍ أَوْ دَيْنٍ
titles.en: Chapter 28 – Some the of the inheritors acknowledge the emancipation or debt

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ غُلَاماً مَمْلُوكاً فَشَهِدَ بَعْضُ الْوَرَثَةِ أَنَّهُ حُرٌّ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ الشَّاهِدُ مَرْضِيّاً جَازَتْ شَهَادَتُهُ فِي نَصِيبِهِ وَ اسْتُسْعِيَ فِيمَا كَانَ لِغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ غُلَاماً مَمْلُوكاً فَشَهِدَ بَعْضُ الْوَرَثَةِ أَنَّهُ حُرٌّ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ الشَّاهِدُ مَرْضِيّاً جَازَتْ شَهَادَتُهُ فِي نَصِيبِهِ وَ اسْتُسْعِيَ فِيمَا كَانَ لِغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ .

segments: Hameed Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from one of his companions, from Aban Bin Usman, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who died and left an owned slave. So one of the inheritors testifies that he is free. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he was present during illness, his testimony is allowed with regards to his share, and he (the slave) would have to work with regards to what was for the others from the inheritors’.
en.hubeali.text:
Hameed Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from one of his companions, from Aban Bin Usman, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who died and left an owned slave. So one of the inheritors testifies that he is free. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he was present during illness, his testimony is allowed with regards to his share, and he (the slave) would have to work with regards to what was for the others from the inheritors’.

segments: 2. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from certain persons of his people from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Mansur ibn Hazim who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who dies and leaves behind an owned slave, but certain ones of the heirs testify that he is free. He (the Imam) said, ‘If the testifying person is an acceptable person his testimony is permissible on his share of the legacy and the slave is made to work to buy his freedom from the rest of the heirs.’”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from certain persons of his people from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Mansur ibn Hazim who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who dies and leaves behind an owned slave, but certain ones of the heirs testify that he is free. He (the Imam) said, ‘If the testifying person is an acceptable person his testimony is permissible on his share of the legacy and the slave is made to work to buy his freedom from the rest of the heirs.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/72)
gradings.label: مرسل
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/72)

narratorChain.raw: حُمَيْدُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب بَعْضِ الْوَرَثَةِ يُقِرُّ بِعِتْقٍ أَوْ دَيْنٍ
titles.en: Chapter 28 – Some the of the inheritors acknowledge the emancipation or debt

segments: رَجُلٍ مَاتَ فَأَقَرَّ عَلَيْهِ بَعْضُ وَرَثَتِهِ لِرَجُلٍ بِدَيْنٍ قَالَ يَلْزَمُهُ ذَلِكَ فِي حِصَّتِهِ .
arabic.text: رَجُلٍ مَاتَ فَأَقَرَّ عَلَيْهِ بَعْضُ وَرَثَتِهِ لِرَجُلٍ بِدَيْنٍ قَالَ يَلْزَمُهُ ذَلِكَ فِي حِصَّتِهِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, form his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, and Husayn Bin Usman, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who died, so one of his inheritors acknowledged a debt over him. He<sup>asws</sup> said, ‘That (the debt) would be necessitated in his share (of inheritance)’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, form his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, and Husayn Bin Usman, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who died, so one of his inheritors acknowledged a debt over him. He<sup>asws</sup> said, ‘That (the debt) would be necessitated in his share (of inheritance)’.

segments: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah and Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who dies and a certain one of his heir confirms his owing a certain amount. He (the Imam) said, ‘It become binding and applicable to his share in the legacy.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah and Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who dies and a certain one of his heir confirms his owing a certain amount. He (the Imam) said, ‘It become binding and applicable to his share in the legacy.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/72)
gradings.label: موثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/72)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ وَ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي
